name: Grottes de Lacan
description: Prehistoric shelters along the Correze river, evidence of habitation at the start of the route.

name: Distillerie Denoix
description: Historic liqueur distillery (est. 1839) producing walnut liqueur and violet mustard. Free tastings.

name: Marche de Brive
description: Major covered market (Tue/Thu/Sat). Famous Foires Grasses truffle and foie gras markets Nov-Feb.

name: Musee Edmond Michelet
description: WWII Resistance museum in the house where Michelet distributed anti-Nazi tracts on June 17, 1940.

name: Domaine de la Gardelle
description: Organic vineyard producing Correze PDO wines including traditional vin paille (straw wine).

name: Grottes de Lamouroux
description: Troglodytic cliff dwellings carved into limestone, inhabited from prehistoric through medieval times.

name: Aubazine Abbey
description: 12th-century Cistercian abbey where Coco Chanel spent six years as an orphan. Geometric patterns may have inspired the Chanel logo.

name: Chateau de Turenne
description: Ruined hilltop keep of the Viscounty of Turenne. Controlled much of Correze until sold to Louis XV in 1738.

name: Eglise Saint-Pierre
description: 11th-century fortified church in Collonges-la-Rouge with carved Romanesque tympanum depicting the Ascension.

name: Castel de Vassinhac
description: Late 16th-century fortified manor in Collonges-la-Rouge with distinctive turrets and loopholes.

name: Maison de la Sirene
description: 16th-century house with sculpted mermaid in Collonges. Once belonged to Henry de Jouvenel, husband of Colette.

name: Beynat Chestnut Festival
description: Annual October Fete de la Chataigne. Chestnut producers, tastings, and summer Tuesday evening markets.

name: Fromagerie Duroux
description: Artisan fromagerie at 12 Route de Chaussenac, Rilhac-Xaintrie (eastern Corrèze, in the Xaintrie). Ages cheese in a reconverted railway tunnel. Produces Pavé Corrézien. Not on the Stage 9 route — closest approach is ~33 km north of the fromagerie at km 50.5.
source: https://www.fromagerie-duroux.fr/ (official site address: 12 Route de Chaussenac, 19220 Rilhac-Xaintrie)

name: Église Saint-Martial de Sainte-Fortunade
description: Parish church holding the relic of Sainte Fortunade, a third-century martyr of Agen whose remains arrived in 894. The early-fifteenth-century reliquary bust still sits in the church.

links.label: Histoire et patrimoine de la commune

name: Château de Sainte-Fortunade
description: Fifteenth-century motte-castle remodelled in the nineteenth century, bought by the commune in 1952 and now housing the mairie. A round tower with machicolations remains.

name: Cascades de Gimel
description: Three waterfalls on the Montane river with a total drop of 143m. Natura 2000 site north of Tulle.

name: Cathedrale Notre-Dame de Tulle
description: 12th-century cathedral with a 73m bell tower, the tallest in the Limousin. Notable carved cloister.

name: Cite de l'Accordeon
description: Opened 2024 in the former Banque de France. Exhibits on accordion-making, Tulle lace, and arms manufacturing.

name: Les Delices de Mel
description: Fromagerie in central Tulle. Regional cheeses including Feuille du Limousin, caillade, and cabecou.

name: Marche de Tulle
description: Wednesday and Saturday morning market at Place Gambetta and Quai Baluze along the Correze river.

name: Tintignac Gallo-Roman Sanctuary
description: Celtic bronze carnyx war trumpets discovered 2004. One of the most important Celtic finds in France. Fanum temple and theatre.

name: Chateau de Bach
description: Castle near Naves with an 18th-century portal and vestiges of a 14th-century Gothic cloister.

name: Chateau de Ventadour
description: 11th-century ruins on a rocky spur. Cradle of troubadour Bernard de Ventadour and Occitan courtly love tradition.

name: Gorges de la Vezere
description: Wild granite gorges of the Vezere river near Treignac. The Saut du Loup rapids are a renowned kayaking spot.

name: Pont Medieval de Treignac
description: 13th/14th-century stone bridge with three arches spanning the Vezere. Iconic view of the medieval town.

name: Eglise Notre-Dame-des-Bans
description: 13th-century church in Treignac built by the Comborn family, remodeled in the 15th century with ribbed vaulting.

name: Lac des Bariousses
description: 99-hectare Blue Flag lake on the Vezere. Swimming, kayak and paddleboard rental in the Monedieres hills.

name: Les Cars Gallo-Roman Site
description: Ruins of two 1st-3rd century Gallo-Roman funerary temples with carved granite on the Plateau de Millevaches.

name: Tourbiere de Longeyroux
description: Largest peat bog in the Limousin (250 hectares, 8,000 years old). Boardwalk trail through heather moors.

name: Lac de Sechemailles
description: 40-hectare Blue Flag lake near Meymac. Swimming, fishing, and hiking trails to Mont Bessou summit.

name: Abbaye Saint-Andre
description: Benedictine abbey founded 1085 in Meymac. Now houses the Centre d'Art Contemporain.

name: Centre d'Art Contemporain
description: Contemporary art center in the Abbaye Saint-Andre. 900m2 across five levels, 3-5 exhibitions per year.

name: Chapelle des Penitents
description: 15th-century funeral chapel in Ussel with a classified Baroque altarpiece. Houses sacred art collection.
source: OSM Nominatim (verified 2026-05-12 during seg 23-27 verification #500). Previous coord (45.548, 2.31) was off by ~370m and incorrectly produced nearest_distance_m 3129 (treating the chapel as 3 km off-route); the Ussel cluster of four attractions shared the same wrong coord.

name: Musee du Pays d'Ussel
description: Classified Musee de France with collections of art, popular traditions, tapestries, and printing materials.
source: OSM Nominatim (verified 2026-05-12 during seg 23-27 verification #500). Previous coord (45.548, 2.3105) was off by ~290m.

name: Marche d'Ussel
description: Saturday morning market in the town center and covered market hall behind the church. Coord (45.548, 2.311) is the original cluster coord shared with three other Ussel attractions; it sits ~250m north of Église Saint-Martin (OSM 45.5470, 2.3091) — plausibly correct for the covered market hall described as 'behind the church', but not OSM-confirmed (recurring event, no fixed OSM node). Other three Ussel attractions in this cluster were re-coorded to OSM-verified values during seg 23-27 verification (#500).
source: Derived km/distance recomputed 2026-05-12 during seg 23-27 verification (#500). Previous stored nearest_km 182.04 / distance 3166 was inconsistent with the lat/lng — the route runs through central Ussel ending at km 184.84, putting the market ~591m off-route at the polyline endpoint.

name: Aigle Romaine
description: 2nd-3rd century granite funerary eagle sculpture at Place Voltaire. Marks the Stage 9 sprint-judge position; the polyline endpoint sits 216m west of the eagle on Avenue Thiers.
source: OSM Nominatim Place Voltaire (verified 2026-05-12 during seg 23-27 verification #500). Previous coord (45.5485, 2.311) was off by ~400m and incorrectly produced nearest_distance_m 3215 (treating the sprint-judge sculpture as 3 km off-route).

name: Huilerie Marty
description: Traditional walnut oil mill in Brive producing cold-pressed huile de noix, a Correze staple.

name: Monument des Martyrs de Tulle
description: Memorial at the Souilhac factory where 99 men were hanged by the SS Das Reich division on June 9, 1944.

name: Manufacture d'Armes de Tulle
description: State arms factory founded 1690; raised to royal manufactory status in 1777 under Louis XVI. Produced the MAT-49 submachine gun. Now partly a cultural space.

name: Maugein Accordeons
description: The last remaining accordion manufacturer in France, founded 1919. Still produces handmade accordions. Visitable workshop.

name: Atelier-Musee des Vieux Metiers
description: Workshop-museum in Collonges-la-Rouge demonstrating traditional Correze trades: woodworking, basketry, and stone carving.

name: Maquis de Chaumeil Memorial
description: Memorial to the Maquis fighters of the Chaumeil area. The remote Monedieres hills sheltered active Resistance cells from 1943-44.

name: Église Saint-Augustin de Saint-Augustin
description: 13th-century parish church at the centre of the Saint-Augustin bourg, the village the Stage 9 route runs through at km 94.5. Inscribed Monument Historique on 28 December 1929. Houses a 17th-century retable carved by Pierre Duhamel of Tulle. The Tourondel hamlet (1.3 km off-route, also in seg 14) is associated with an old wooden chapel that locally is said to have inspired the church's nave vault.
source: https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint-Augustin_(Corr%C3%A8ze) and Mérimée database PA00099811 (verified 2026-05-07). Coord = bourg centre per OSM place=village; the church sits in the bourg square.

name: Suc au May Table d'orientation
description: Panoramic table d'orientation at the 908m summit of the Suc au May, the highest point of the Monédières range. The oldest table d'orientation in the Limousin, inaugurated 25 August 1935 by Henry de Jouvenel — the same Henry de Jouvenel who lived at the Maison de la Sirène in Collonges-la-Rouge (seg 4). On clear days the panorama reaches the Monts d'Auvergne to the east, the Plateau de Millevaches to the north, and the Bas-Limousin valleys to the south. A 15-minute footpath climbs to the summit from a small parking area on the D26 just below the road's high point. Modern paragliding launch site. The road itself peaks ~440m short of the actual summit at km 105.15 (elev 889m on the road, 908m at the summit).
source: https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suc_au_May (verified 2026-05-07; Henry de Jouvenel inauguration date cross-checked via Tourisme Egletons). Callback to seg 4 Maison de la Sirène (Henry de Jouvenel + Colette).

name: Musée du Président Jacques Chirac
description: Museum at Sarran, opened June 2000, housing the official gifts received by Jacques Chirac during his presidency (1995-2007). Sarran was the Chirac family home; Chirac is buried in the Sarran communal cemetery. The route passes 3.2 km west of Sarran on the climb out of Saint-Augustin toward Chaumeil; not on-route but adjacent. Pairs with Ussel (seg 25-26), where Chirac began his political career as a municipal councillor in 1965.
source: https://www.museepresidentjcchirac.fr/ and Wikipédia (verified 2026-05-07). 3.17 km off route is within the precedent set by Cascades de Gimel (4.3 km, kept in PR #470).

name: Stele des Maquisards
description: Monument in Bugeat commemorating the Maquis du Mont Gargan and Plateau de Millevaches partisans.

name: Pont de la Tourmente
description: Medieval fortified bridge in Treignac over the Vezere. Site of Hundred Years' War skirmishes when English forces raided the Bas-Limousin.

name: La Table de Jean
description: Restaurant in Meymac featuring regional cuisine: pounti (herb and meat terrine), millassou (corn cake), and Millevaches chestnuts.

name: Eglise Saint-Vincent de Meyssac
description: 12th-16th century church with Romanesque Limousin-style portal, fortified during the Hundred Years War. Red sandstone bell tower with white limestone tympanum — both sides of the Meyssac fault in one building.

name: Halle aux Grains de Meyssac
description: 18th-century grain hall with red sandstone columns supporting a Travassac slate roof on a chestnut timber frame. The geological fault made architectural.

name: Espace de Decouverte de la Faille de Meyssac
description: Discovery centre at Noailhac interpreting the Meyssac geological fault. Nine illustrated panels, fossils, rock samples, and a scale model. Free admission.

name: Église Notre-Dame-de-la-Nativité, Lagleygeolle
description: Village church of Lagleygeolle, carrying vestiges from the 12th, 15th, and 16th centuries; the bell tower is from 1889. The commune's Occitan name (la gleisòla, "the small church") derives from the priory that became this church.

name: Cabane de la Fée (dolmen)
description: Neolithic dolmen in Beynat commune, listed as a Historic Monument since 1910. Off the route, reached via Route du Dolmen.

name: Commanderie de Puy de Noix
description: Twelfth-century Templar commandery in Beynat commune, on a wooded hill less than a kilometre off the route. Acquired by the Knights Templar in the late 12th century; passed to the Hospitallers of Saint John after the order's suppression in 1312.

links.label: Espace historique de Puy de Noix

name: Dolmen de Rochesseux
description: Neolithic dolmen in Aubazine commune; two orthostates and a covering slab restored to position in 1944. Construction estimated to the 3rd millennium BCE.

name: Dolmen du Puy de la Ramière
description: Neolithic collective burial in Noailhac commune; supporting slabs remain, covering disappeared. On the Sentier des Dolmens hiking trail. Excavations from 1865.

name: Dolmen de la Chassagne
description: Neolithic dolmen at Saint-Cernin-de-Larche, on a burial mound 18 m in diameter. Caussenarde Neolithic culture. Classified historic monument 1988 (Mérimée PA00099844).

name: Prieuré Saint-Michel-des-Anges de Saint-Angel
description: Fortified Benedictine priory in Saint-Angel village. The fortified church, conventual buildings, and round presbytery tower together were classified as Monument Historique in 1840 — one of the earliest classifications in France, on the original list compiled by Prosper Mérimée. The priory sits in seg 26 (km 176.60, 153m off the polyline).
source: fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint-Angel_(Corrèze) (verified 2026-05-12 during seg 23-27 verification #500). Coord from Wikipédia commune infobox. Saint-Angel and the priory were absent from attractions.json before this audit; village added to data/segments.json seg 26 towns and to town-coords.json in the same PR.

name: Stade Alexandre-Cueille
description: Tulle's main stadium on Boulevard Auzelou, inaugurated 18 August 1929. Home of the Sporting club tulliste rugby team; also has an athletics track. Stage 9 of the 2026 Tour de France passes the stadium at km 70.9 as the route leaves Tulle northbound. Annually the departure point of L'Agglomérée cyclosportive (organised by Tulle Cyclisme Compétition), which in 2026 covered 40 km of the actual Stage 9 route including Suc au May.
source: https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stade_Alexandre-Cueille (verified 2026-05-06; lat/lng from OSM cross-check at boulevard Auzelou, Tulle)
